nts1.d: An hour deep dive into the women who have recorded at the world famous Studio One, featuring Jennifer Lara, Cherry Ann, Rita Marley + more.
Straight from the 45s, a selection of Mexican female garage rock & yeyé recorded in Mexico between 1964 and 1970.
This program is curated by Ondas Representative, Dubby. It features artists, both well-known and unknown, with a unique aesthetic from a wide range of genres that used to be available at Ondas.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ SPIRIT BLUE & Theadora
nts1.d: Dawn moods for the early birds, courtesy of Sleepier & Impey aka Spirit Blue.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.

nts1.n: SOUP TO NUTS W/ SHY ONE
nts1.d: DJ & producer Shy One oversees the chatroom nonsense and brings that necessary get-up-and-go vibe every single hump day…

nts1.n: Tropic of Love w/ Mafalda & Pedro Ricardo
nts1.d: Vibrant eclecticism from Portuguese selector Mafalda, for two hours every month.
Parisian party instigator Louise Chen pulls from a timeless well of classic & modern house, disco, soul, pop, & more.

In a prolific era in the late 1980s and early 1990s, New Jersey Twin brothers Reginald (aka Rheji) and Ronald (aka Rhano) Burrell laid upon the early Chicago foundations of deep house with a stack of influential releases under various monikers that would come to define the sound of the iconic Nu Groove label. They channeled their eclectic influences – gospel, americana, new jack swing, hip hop and more into hypnotising dance tracks.
Selected and mixed by Finn.

nts1.n: Can You Feel The Sun w/ Parris
The Lunacy of Flowers w/ Dave ID (R)
THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ PAM
THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
DANCE MUSIC SHOW W/ BELL TOWERS

nts2.n: Low Life Loves You w/ Bill Brewster (R)
nts2.d: Writer, historian and DJ Bill Brewster showcasing two hours of love, laughter, and music.
Horsegirl Sounds Radio Hour is a long running playlist curated by Penelope, Gigi, and Nora. Now they bring their monthly selections to the NTS airwaves.
Canadian record label, publisher, and distributors Séance Centre conjure timeless music from the past, present, and future.
Death Is Not The End digs up the oldest of the old, serving up your gospel, blues and folk with a crackle. Hear the dust.
The underground and experimental hero, composer, and guitar abstractionist Oren Ambarchi invites us to regular eclectic listening sessions, exploring the breadth of his extensive record collection.
Regular transmissions from the Japanese songwriter & composer Eiko Ishibashi, sharing favourites traversing folk, jazz, avant-garde composition and more.

Rose-tinted hallucinogenic sounds from Astral Vibes' Acid Memories. Illicit dealings in psychedelia of all persuasions. Sit back and enjoy…
From the host: "This mix is dedicated to vintage synths, cosmic library sounds, and rose-tinted frequencies.
A slow, sensual orbit through forgotten studio experiments and psychedelic grooves.
Floating somewhere between the body, memory, and deep space.
Sit back… and enjoy the journey.
this is Acid Memories !"

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "A journey through Estonian film music (1967–1986) — from orchestral scores and jazz-infused soundtracks to early electronic experiments. Featuring works by Sven Grünberg, Raimo Kangro, Rein Rannap, and others, written for Estonian, Ukrainian, and Polish films, animation, and television."

80's Rampwalk delivers an hour of cold wave, minimal synth, industrial, post-punk and music made in Berlin basements.
From the host: "Avian society / perfumed bordello / a stranger under a lamp post in a trench coat and borsalino hat / "She drank from a boiling kettle" / Distributed Denial of Service Attack / jilted at the altar (a dead cert at the hounds) / a pale shape floats across an otherwise empty dance floor."

nts2.n: FIFTH WORLD W/ IAN KIM JUDD
nts2.d:
A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.
From the host: "Thaw tracks with IKJ. First solo mix of the year, marking the ninth (!?) year of the show. Thanks as always for listening."
Jen Monroe writes the album download blog Listen To This!, which focuses on Japanese and new age records: Listen to This

nts2.n: HEAVEN AND EARTH MAGIC W/ Barbarelle
Beyond The Clouds w/ Nevena Jeremic
Club Aerobics w/ Bianca Oblivion & AOBEATS
On the Road w/ Adelina S - Gris-gris tour pt. 1

subtle.d: Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

subtle.n: THE CULTURE HEAD w. SAINT VOX
Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Hannah Rubin and Noelle Armstrong – MELLOW DRAMA
Frankie Jax No Mad - Not Evil Radio
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
